companies.problem_solved: Healthcare organizations face inefficient procurement processes, lack of purchasing power, and difficulties in managing logistics for medical supplies and equipment. Resah provides a centralized procurement platform that helps hospitals, EHPADs (nursing homes), and other healthcare facilities optimize their purchasing and logistics operations.
companies.who_buys_it: Public and private non-profit healthcare organizations including hospitals, EHPADs (French nursing homes), home care services (HAD, SAD), health centers, and local authorities in France.
key_features: Centralized procurement platform for medical supplies and equipment
Negotiated contracts with suppliers for better pricing
Logistics management and supply chain optimization
Resource center for procurement best practices
Label RFAR certified for responsible supplier relations
Support for sustainable and ethical purchasing
Training and professional development for procurement teams
companies.business_model: B2B membership model. Healthcare organizations join as members to access the procurement platform and services. Revenue comes from membership fees and negotiated supplier agreements. Public procurement framework compliant with French regulations.
companies.why_varden_should_care: Resah operates at the intersection of healthcare procurement and digital transformation in France's healthcare system. With France's aging population driving demand for elderly care facilities and services, Resah's platform is critical infrastructure for cost management and operational efficiency in the sector. The company has strong government backing (first public health actor to receive RFAR certification) and serves as a gateway to the entire French healthcare procurement ecosystem.

companies.problem_solved: Home care organizations and nursing homes struggle with fragmented care processes, inefficient data exchange between healthcare providers, and lack of integrated care management tools. Nedap Ons provides comprehensive care management software that supports the entire care process and enables standardized data exchange across healthcare networks.
companies.who_buys_it: Home care organizations, nursing homes, residential care facilities, and healthcare networks in the Netherlands and Belgium.
key_features: Integrated care management software for home care and nursing homes
Standardized data exchange with other healthcare providers
Care planning and scheduling tools
Client/patient management
Mobile application for care workers
Compliance with Dutch healthcare standards and regulations
Real-time reporting and analytics
Interoperability with other healthcare information systems
companies.business_model: B2B SaaS model. Subscription-based licensing to healthcare organizations. Revenue from software licenses, implementation services, and ongoing support. Part of Nedap N.V., a publicly traded Dutch technology company with €210M+ annual revenue.
companies.why_varden_should_care: Nedap Ons is part of Nedap Healthcare, a division of a well-established Dutch technology company with a strong track record in healthcare technology. The Netherlands has one of Europe's most advanced elderly care systems, and Nedap Ons is a key player in digitizing care delivery. Their focus on standardized data exchange positions them well for the growing trend toward integrated care networks and cross-provider collaboration in elderly care.

companies.problem_solved: Family members and caregivers struggle to stay informed about their elderly relatives' care status, coordinate care activities among multiple stakeholders, and communicate effectively with healthcare professionals. Caren provides a digital platform that connects family members, caregivers, and healthcare professionals to improve care coordination and communication.
companies.who_buys_it: Family members caring for elderly relatives, informal caregivers, and healthcare organizations that want to improve communication with families and care coordination. Over 719,000 active users and 1,290 connected healthcare organizations.
key_features: Digital care coordination platform for families and caregivers
Real-time updates on care activities and well-being
Calendar for organizing care appointments and tasks
Secure messaging with healthcare professionals
Task assignment and delegation among family members
Integration with professional care organizations
Privacy-controlled data sharing
Mobile app for on-the-go access
companies.business_model: Freemium B2C/B2B model. Free basic accounts for family members. Healthcare organizations pay for integration and premium features. Revenue from healthcare organization subscriptions and premium features. Over 1,290 connected organizations as paying customers.
companies.why_varden_should_care: Caren addresses a critical pain point in elderly care - the communication gap between professional caregivers and family members. With the growing trend toward 'aging in place' and the increasing role of informal caregivers, platforms that facilitate care coordination are becoming essential infrastructure. Caren's strong user base (700K+ users) and network effects create a defensible position in the Dutch market.

companies.problem_solved: Healthcare organizations, insurance companies, and life sciences companies face challenges in managing patient data, streamlining healthcare processes, and complying with complex regulatory requirements. Cegedim provides comprehensive IT solutions and services across the healthcare value chain, including solutions specifically designed for elderly care management.
companies.who_buys_it: Healthcare professionals (doctors, nurses, pharmacists), health insurance companies, mutual insurance companies, pension funds, life sciences companies, and HR departments in healthcare organizations.
key_features: Electronic health record systems for healthcare professionals
Practice management software for medical practices
Claims processing and management for insurance companies
Patient relationship management tools
Data analytics and business intelligence for healthcare
Regulatory compliance solutions
Healthcare supply chain management
Telemedicine and remote care solutions
companies.business_model: B2B SaaS and services model. Revenue from software licenses, implementation services, transaction processing fees, and ongoing support. Publicly traded company (Euronext Paris: CGM) with €500M+ annual revenue. Diversified revenue streams across healthcare ecosystem.
companies.why_varden_should_care: Cegedim is a well-established player in the European healthcare IT market with 50+ years of experience and a strong presence in France and internationally. Their solutions span the entire healthcare value chain, from primary care to insurance and life sciences. For Varden, Cegedim represents a mature, profitable company with deep expertise in healthcare technology and strong relationships across the French healthcare system.

companies.problem_solved: Referring patients to the right healthcare provider is often inefficient, with phone calls, faxing, and manual processes leading to delays and errors. ZorgDomein provides a digital platform for healthcare referrals and care coordination, enabling healthcare providers to efficiently refer patients to specialists, hospitals, and other care providers.
companies.who_buys_it: General practitioners, medical specialists, hospitals, home care organizations, mental health providers, and other healthcare organizations in the Netherlands.
key_features: Digital referral platform for healthcare providers
Real-time availability and waiting times of care providers
Standardized referral protocols
Integration with electronic health records
Care coordination tools
Patient communication features
Reporting and analytics on referral patterns
Compliance with Dutch healthcare regulations
companies.business_model: B2B SaaS model. Healthcare organizations pay subscription fees to use the platform. Revenue from software licenses and transaction fees. Established market position as the leading referral platform in the Netherlands.
companies.why_varden_should_care: ZorgDomein plays a critical role in the Dutch healthcare system as the backbone of patient referrals and care coordination. With the increasing focus on integrated care and the need to efficiently route elderly patients to appropriate care providers, platforms like ZorgDomein are becoming essential infrastructure. The network effects from connecting primary care providers with specialists create strong competitive advantages.

companies.problem_solved: Elderly individuals and those with chronic conditions need comprehensive care and support that spans home care, residential care, and rehabilitation. Sensire provides integrated care services combined with innovative digital solutions like MIJNSensire to help people live independently and safely at home for longer.
companies.who_buys_it: Individuals needing care services (elderly, chronically ill, rehabilitation patients), family members, and local governments/municipalities that fund care services in the Netherlands.
key_features: Home nursing and care services (wijkverpleging)
Residential care facilities (20+ locations in East Gelderland)
Rehabilitation and recovery programs
MIJNSensire - smart digital care solutions for independent living
24/7 care support
Personalized care planning
Integration with family caregivers
Specialized dementia care programs
companies.business_model: B2C/B2G model. Revenue from care services funded by Dutch healthcare insurance and local government budgets. MIJNSensire digital solutions are offered as part of care packages. Largest care organization in East Gelderland with comprehensive regional coverage.
companies.why_varden_should_care: Sensire represents the evolution of traditional care organizations into tech-enabled care providers. Their MIJNSensire initiative demonstrates how care organizations are adopting digital solutions to extend their reach and improve outcomes for elderly patients. This model of combining physical care delivery with digital tools is increasingly important as healthcare systems look to scale care for aging populations.

companies.problem_solved: Families struggle to find reliable, qualified home care assistants for elderly relatives, and coordinating care services is complex and time-consuming. Ouihelp provides a platform that matches families with vetted home care assistants and manages the entire care process from scheduling to payment and administrative tasks.
companies.who_buys_it: Families with elderly relatives needing home care assistance, individuals with disabilities, and people experiencing loss of autonomy. Over 7,000 families served across France.
key_features: Vetted and qualified home care assistants (auxiliaires de vie)
Dedicated care advisor for personalized support
24-hour service setup capability
Comprehensive home care services (personal care, household tasks, companionship)
Administrative support (paperwork, financing assistance)
Local agencies across France for personalized service
Consistent care worker assignments for relationship continuity
Integration with French social benefits (APA, tax credits)
companies.business_model: B2C marketplace model. Revenue from hourly care services with families paying for care hours. Strong integration with French social benefits system (50% tax credit, APA funding). Network of local agencies for personalized service. Focus on quality and reliability to differentiate from informal care market.
companies.why_varden_should_care: Ouihelp is addressing the massive 'silver economy' market in France, where aging populations are driving demand for home care services. While primarily a service provider, their technology platform for matching, scheduling, and managing care workers represents an interesting model for scaling home care. The integration with French social benefits creates a compelling value proposition for families navigating the complex French care financing system.

companies.problem_solved: Patients with chronic conditions and elderly individuals need convenient access to medical supplies, pharmaceuticals, and home care products. Mediq provides a comprehensive healthcare platform that delivers pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and care products directly to patients' homes, combined with pharmacy services and clinical support.
companies.who_buys_it: Patients with chronic conditions, elderly individuals, healthcare professionals, hospitals, and pharmacies across 14 European countries.
key_features: Direct-to-patient pharmaceutical and medical supply delivery
Home care products and medical devices
Clinical pharmacy services
Digital ordering and prescription management
Specialized programs for chronic disease management
Integration with healthcare providers
Sustainable and responsible business practices (ESG focused)
Strong presence in Benelux and broader Europe
companies.business_model: B2C/B2B hybrid model. Revenue from pharmaceutical and medical supply sales, pharmacy services, and contracts with healthcare systems. Founded in 1899 as a pharmacist cooperative, now operates across 14 countries. Strong focus on operational excellence and digital technology for care delivery.
companies.why_varden_should_care: Mediq represents the pharmaceutical and medical supply side of elderly care, which is often overlooked but critical to enabling aging in place. Their direct-to-patient model and digital infrastructure for ordering and delivery are essential for elderly patients who may have difficulty accessing pharmacies. The company's 120+ year history and pan-European presence provide stability and scale in the healthcare supply chain.

notes.market_context: France and Benelux have among the most developed elderly care systems in Europe, driven by aging populations and strong social welfare systems. France has over 12 million people aged 65+, with EHPADs (nursing homes) serving 600,000+ residents. The Netherlands is a leader in home-based care, with 80% of elderly care delivered at home.
key_trends: Aging in place: Strong government and societal preference for home-based care over institutional care
Digital transformation: Rapid adoption of care management software and telehealth solutions
Workforce shortages: Care sector facing significant staff shortages, driving automation and efficiency solutions
Integrated care: Push toward better coordination between primary care, specialists, and long-term care providers
Family caregiver support: Growing recognition of informal caregivers' role and need for tools to support them
challenges: Fragmented market with many small regional players
Complex reimbursement systems (especially in France with APA, tax credits, etc.)
Resistance to technology adoption among some care providers
Budget constraints in publicly funded care systems
notes.search_limitations: Web search functionality was severely limited during this research, resulting in difficulty finding and verifying company details. Some company information is based on prior knowledge of the elderly care technology landscape in France and Benelux.
